methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $120M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.name: World Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Nominal
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B

private.name: Photoroom
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Hippocratic AI, a Series B-stage AI & Software company founded by Munjal Shah, is evaluated against a comp universe of 5 same-stage private peers in the AI sector (Safe Superintelligence, Physical Intelligence, World Labs, Nominal, and Photoroom) alongside 1 public comparable (Palantir Technologies).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the company's $120M total raised as a proxy base value and applying a typical 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, yields $228M. Peer-median analysis across the 5 same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $5.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $32.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects significant variance in AI company valuations at this stage, driven by differences in capital efficiency, go-to-market traction, and perceived technical differentiation.

Triangulating these methods supports a valuation range of $194M (floor) to $5.8B (ceiling), with a median estimate of $2.6B. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound, while peer-median valuation dominates the mid-to-high range. Key uncertainties include revenue or unit economics data, which would refine positioning within the peer range, and the applicability of the $32B Safe Superintelligence valuation as an outlier versus a market signal. The substantial gap between methods reflects early-stage information constraints typical of Series B AI companies.
